name: Default Flow — Rainbow/Orange Setup
basedOn: Ultimate Wynncraft Lootrun Guide, Run Setup + Run Combos sections
confidence: Beacon orderings are inherited from a pre-2.2.1 snapshot. Every `aqua` position is marked `disputed` pending the additive-boosting measurement.

runConstants.dailyBonusSource: data/ranks.json `dailyBonus` — canonical. Do NOT duplicate the values here: the +1 Reward Reroll is gated at Elite II and doubles with Silverbull, so a flat copy would be wrong at most ranks.
runConstants.tokenAcquisition: 6 tokens for 4 Shares (currency). Per user: OUT OF SCOPE for the advisor — treat tokens held as a run-setup input, not something the advisor plans toward.

runnable.note: Weak/random generators (Jester's Trick, Complete Chaos) do not satisfy this alone.

invariants.note: Checked every challenge, in every phase. Violations outrank phase priority.

safety.why: Timeout risk, and GREEN SELF-EXCLUDES — a green you decline is gone next challenge. This is take-now-or-lose-it, not a ranking preference.
safety.correctedFrom: Earlier rationale said 'green costs nothing because it returns next challenge' — exactly backwards. Green is one of only two self-excluding beacons (with red), so declining it forfeits it. Aqua and orange are the ones that freely return.

disabledBy.reason: Cannot gain time for 12 challenges — the guard is INOPERATIVE. Warn loudly instead of recommending green.

safety.why: Below the 4-challenge buffer the run ends before any combo can pay off.

safety.uiWarning: HUBRIS ACTIVE — /kill or any death ends the run immediately.

safety.why: Does nothing on the final challenge.
safety.detail: Guide (Misc. Info): on the final challenge red/white beacons and purple/darkGrey pulls still work, while yellow, grey, crimson and blue beacons, plus new curses, Chronokinesis, Sacrificial Ritual and Gambling Beast procs, all do nothing.
safety.correctedFrom: Blue was missing from this list — the advisor would have recommended a dead Blue Beacon on the last challenge.

phases.name: Opening
phases.intent: Buy optionality before anything else. Beacon choices are the input to every later decision.

escapeHatch.rule: A run can be RESET by failing before challenge 4. Sacrificed pulls are retained; a 15-minute cooldown applies.
escapeHatch.why: This makes a bad opening cheap to abandon. If the first three challenges produce no orange/aqua/pink and the pool looks hostile, resetting can beat playing out a doomed run — the advisor should offer it as an explicit option, not just rank beacons.
escapeHatch.constraint: Only available before challenge 4, so the decision window is exactly this phase.

notes: Orange is the safest top pick post-2.2.1: it adds choices and is untouched by the boosting rework.
DISPUTED: aqua's rank here assumes multiplicative stacking. If additive is broad, aqua likely drops below pink and red.

phases.name: Mission Gate (forced)

gate.rule: DO NOT fulfil the mission until a comfortable run extension is secured.
gate.why: Committing an archetype before you have the challenges to execute it wastes the slot.
gate.deferral: Grey beacons can be skipped roughly 10 times, so deferring is cheap and usually correct.

missionPolicy.note: Only ~3 mission slots exist, so this pick effectively selects the archetype. See data/archetypes.json.
missionPolicy.fallback: If no archetype core is offered, take a `universal` mission (High Roller / Redemption) — never dead.

phases.stacking: Vibrant Aqua -> Vibrant Grey yields 5 mission choices. Worth setting up if the grey can be deferred.

phases.name: Run Extension
phases.intent: Convert the 12 starting challenges into a runway long enough for an archetype to pay off.

hardRule.rule: Take rainbow on sight. Never skip it.
hardRule.why: Rainbow uses a ramping hazard rate after challenge 10 — the chance climbs until it appears, then resets. Skipping forfeits an event you cannot re-roll into cheaply.

rerollPolicy.action: reroll to force orange or rainbow
rerollPolicy.note: 2.2.1 makes early forcing weak — at challenge 11 the rainbow hazard is still low. Rerolling for rainbow gets better the longer it has been absent.

phases.name: Trial Prep
phases.intent: Hold an aqua into challenge 20 — 'aqua boosted crimson beacons are a near must'.

crimsonPolicy.why: Okay to skip once or twice to set up an aqua stack. Crimson is capped at 2 uses, so quality beats speed.

trialPreference.firstTrialRule: Ultimate Sacrifice is the recommended FIRST trial while mob scaling is still low.
trialPreference.secondTrialRule: Gambling Beast should almost always be the SECOND trial — sustain it by stacking vibrant aqua/green. It grants no reroll if the timer cannot absorb the cost.
trialPreference.note: Archetype `trialPreference` refines this ordering but must not promote anything in `neverTake`.

phases.name: Mission Completion Deadline

deadline.rule: Spend all 3 grey beacons before challenge 40.
deadline.why: Grey stops appearing around challenge 30-50. An unspent mission slot at 40 is a permanently wasted third of your mission capacity.

phases.escalation: Raise grey priority as challenge 40 approaches and unspent slots remain.

phases.name: Farm (good combo)
phases.intent: Extend toward challenge 100 and cycle the combo.
manage: Sustain boons via aqua-stacked vibrant blue for 400% potency

phases.name: Salvage (no combo)
phases.intent: Cash out a failed run rather than limp to the end.
plan: FIRST: try the comboless pull line — Porphyrophobia + Route Indigo yields up to 300+ pulls from an otherwise dead run. Non-terminal, so attempt before any cash-out.
Other comboless-viable missions: Redemption, High Roller, Requiem, Radiant Hunter, Chronokinesis. Lights Out is the most reliable passive (200+ pulls).
Stack curses maximally (Madman caps at 8; keep >= 8 active).

NOTE: Gambling Beast is NOT salvage-only. The guide rates it A tier and 'should almost always be taken as 2nd trial', sustained by stacking vibrant aqua/green. It belongs in the normal trial plan, not just here.
Alternative cash-out: Treasury Bill for +75% of current pulls — better when the pull bank is already large.
phases.ordering: Non-terminal salvage (Porphyrophobia + Route Indigo, curse stacking) MUST be exhausted before terminal cash-outs (Gambling Beast). A cash-out forecloses the rest of the run; the pull line does not.
phases.why: Gambling Beast is only correct here. As a normal pick it is run-ending; as a deliberate cash-out it is the best salvage available.

phases.name: Endgame
notes: Knife Edge pays 7 minus challengesRemaining — worthless earlier, strong here.
Red priority rises: challenges, not time, are now the binding constraint.
On the final challenge suppress everything in safety.last-challenge-suppression.

rerollPolicy.note: Gourmand's rate (+1 or +2 choices per reroll) is DISPUTED and materially changes this policy.

boonPolicy.note: Dynamic boons favour early pickup, static boons favour late. See data/boons.json `kind`.
boonPolicy.madmanRule: Hold 6-8 curses before taking Madman; it snapshots and caps at 8.
boonPolicy.metaNote: Guide recommends prioritising EHP boons given current meta damage output — a survivability concern the state machine cannot model. Expose as a user preference.
boonPolicy.potencyCeiling: Aqua-stacked Vibrant Blue yields boons at 400% potency.

tactics.note: Cross-phase tactics from playtest feedback (Mujtaba, 2026-07-24). These layer on top of phase priority and archetype bias.

boostedOnly.why: High-value beacons are not worth taking raw — people want them aqua/vibrant for max power. Taking an unboosted one wastes the single use (white/darkGrey) or the option count (grey).
boostedOnly.firstRainbowWhy: The FIRST rainbow is worth taking raw — it makes everything vibrant, so getting it online early pays for itself. Later ones should be stacked.

aquaLoop.why: The optimised steady state is alternating aqua with the payoff beacon your combo needs: aqua->blue->aqua->blue for a boon combo, aqua->green->aqua->green for time, purple for curses, yellow for flying chests.

orangeRefresh.why: Refresh orange BEFORE it lapses — losing beacon choices shrinks every later decision.

missionUrgency.why: Do not PROMOTE a raw grey — more mission choices means a better combo, and greys are skippable ~10x. Only push an unboosted grey once the window is closing and waiting is no longer affordable.
missionUrgency.windowNote: Grey stops appearing around challenge 30, so 25 leaves a few offers of slack. TUNABLE — set to taste.

priorityTokensNote: A priority entry may be a plain colour or a boost-qualified one (buffed:/aqua:/boosted:/vibrant:). A boosted entry matches only when the beacon resolves above tier 0 (aqua bank OR rainbow), and outranks the plain entry. This expresses "set up aqua, then take the boosted payoff" directly in the list — aqua sits high, but a boosted white/rainbow sits higher still.
